topic: My dog barks at the exact same spot in the kitchen at the exact same time every night
title: My Dog Barks at the Exact Same Spot in the Kitchen at Eleven Forty Seven Every Night | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My dog barks at the same spot on the kitchen floor every night at eleven forty seven.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a dim suburban kitchen at night, a golden retriever standing alert facing a section of tile floor near the pantry door, his ears up and tail rigid, a single overhead pendant light casting a yellow pool on the floor, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Small ranch house. Outside Dallas. Six years in this kitchen.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of a small one story brick ranch house at dusk, a covered carport on one side, mowed lawn in front, a single living room window glowing warm orange, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: He is a labrador. Eight years old. Calm dog.
scenes.image_prompt: medium shot of a black labrador lying on a beige tile kitchen floor in the afternoon, the dog at rest with his head on his paws, sunlight streaming through a partially open window blind behind, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Started three weeks after my wife moved out. Same square of tile every time.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a kitchen tile floor seen from above, a single off white twelve inch tile slightly discolored at its grout edges, the surrounding tiles uniform, dim overhead light casting soft shadow at the edges, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: He growls first. Then bark. Then he backs into the hallway.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a labrador slowly backing through an open kitchen doorway into a dim hallway, his eyes still fixed on a section of tile floor in front of him, the kitchen counters lit dimly behind him, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I checked the spot. Tile. Subfloor. Concrete slab underneath.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a section of beige kitchen tile partially pried up with a putty knife on the floor beside it, the exposed plywood subfloor visible underneath, a battery work light illuminating the small hole, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I waited up with him last Tuesday.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a dim kitchen at night seen from a hallway doorway, a man seated on a wooden kitchen chair near the table with his back to the camera, a black labrador beside him on the tile floor, single overhead light dimly on, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Eleven forty seven. He barked. I heard knocking from below the floor.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a beige kitchen tile floor in dim light, the surface unchanged but the perspective angled low and tense, the wooden kitchen chair leg visible at the edge of the frame, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Three knocks. Then a pause. Then three more.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a beige kitchen floor tile from a kneeling perspective, an adult ear pressed against the cool surface, the rest of the kitchen blurred behind in dim warm light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The previous owner died in this kitchen. The neighbor told me yesterday.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of a residential front lawn in late afternoon, an older woman in a sun hat standing on her own porch next door talking toward the camera perspective, the small ranch house porch visible in foreground, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Heart attack. He fell on that exact tile. They found him three days later.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a kitchen floor tile with afternoon sunlight cutting a sharp rectangle across it from a window, dust motes visible in the beam, the grout lines around the tile slightly darker, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My dog will not come into the kitchen anymore. Last night the knocking answered me back.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of an empty dim kitchen at night seen from the hallway doorway, the tile floor empty, no dog visible, the pendant light off, only ambient light from a neighboring room casting faint shadows, vertical 9:16 framing
